title: LORA_DEV macro expands to DEVICE_DT_GET with no existence check
description:
lora_l2.c:46: #define LORA_DEV DEVICE_DT_GET(DT_ALIAS(lora0))

If lora0 alias doesn't exist in devicetree, this produces a compile error. But the error message is cryptic (something about undefined symbol).

What goes wrong: A user building for a board without lora0 alias gets a linker error like 'undefined reference to __device_dts_ord_XX' with no indication that the problem is a missing LoRa device in devicetree.

Fix: Use DT_NODE_EXISTS(DT_ALIAS(lora0)) guard or BUILD_ASSERT with clear message. Example pattern from Zephyr samples:
  BUILD_ASSERT(DT_NODE_EXISTS(DT_ALIAS(lora0)), "LoRa device alias 'lora0' not defined in devicetree");
close_reason: Fixed: Added BUILD_ASSERT with DT_NODE_EXISTS guard before LORA_DEV macro. Users without lora0 devicetree alias now get a clear compile-time error message explaining the problem and how to fix it.
